examples.title: Simple Oracle Data Request Pattern
examples.description: Basic pattern showing how a smart contract requests data from an oracle and handles the callback.
examples.explanation: This demonstrates the request-fulfill pattern common in oracle systems. The smart contract initiates a request with a unique ID, and the oracle later calls back with the data using that same ID for verification.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for request, O(1) for callback
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of pending requests stored

examples.title: Decentralized Oracle Consensus Algorithm
examples.description: Shows how multiple oracle nodes report data and the contract calculates consensus using median value.
examples.explanation: This code shows consensus determination in decentralized oracles. Multiple oracle nodes submit signed reports, signatures are verified for authenticity, and the median value is calculated to determine the final price, filtering out outliers.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) due to sorting

examples.title: Oracle-Based Insurance Trigger
examples.description: Demonstrates a real-world smart oracle application: automatic insurance claim triggers based on external sensor data.
examples.explanation: This shows a real-world smart oracle use case: parametric insurance. The contract requests weather data, and when oracle fulfills with temperature/humidity data exceeding thresholds, payout is automatically triggered. Demonstrates oracle executing logic (checking conditions) not just data retrieval.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for purchase, O(1) for fulfillment
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of active policies
